description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a robust, symmetrical design, featuring two large, crescent-shaped blades on either side of a central shaft. The blades are decorated with intricate, gold-toned Celtic knot patterns and bordered by a metallic, slightly serrated edge, suggesting both ornamental and functional qualities. The central hub where the blades meet the handle is reinforced with metallic bands and rivets, adding to its durability and visual complexity. The handle is cylindrical, wrapped in a textured, dark brown material, and capped at both ends with metallic fittings, the lower of which includes a ring for hanging or securing the axe. The overall color palette consists of dark steel, bronze, and brown tones, giving it an aged, battle-worn appearance. This axe would be visually striking in an urban fantasy or historical context, possibly as a display piece or ceremonial tool.
description: A double-headed battle axe with ornate gold Celtic patterns on wide, crescent blades and a reinforced, textured handle capped with metal fittings.
description_view_0: Front view showing both decorated blades, central hub, and full handle detail.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the symmetrical blade design and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle alignment.
attribute_handle_type: Cylindrical, straight handle wrapped in textured leather or similar material, with metallic caps at both ends and a ring at the base for hanging.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, crescent-shaped blades with wide cutting edges, intricate gold Celtic knot engravings, and a slightly serrated outer edge.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged and banded mounting with visible rivets and reinforced metallic hub.
